  facet_wrap(~Microniche, ncol = 5) +
  theme_bw()

PCoA_plot_all_microniche_claude

ggsave("PCoA_plot_microniche_PR.pdf", plot = PCoA_plot_all_microniche_claude, width = 8, height = 4, dpi = 300)



```
#Plot alpha diversity across the gut and microniches
```{r}
#Plot  alpha diversity for each region. Here you should use unnormalized data.
alpha_diversity_shannon_biogeography <- plot_richness(btphylo_atsacrifice, x="Biogeography", color = "Treatment", measures=c("Shannon"))+
  geom_boxplot()+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"))+
  labs(y = "Alpha Diversity - Shannon", x = "Biogeography", color = "Treatment Group")+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 0.5, hjust=0.5))
  

alpha_diversity_shannon_biogeography

alpha_diversity_simpson_biogeography <- plot_richness(btphylo_atsacrifice, x="Biogeography", color = "Treatment", measures=c("Simpson"))+
  geom_boxplot()+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"))+
  labs(y = "Alpha Diversity - Simpson", x = "Biogeography", color = "Treatment Group")+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 0.5, hjust=0.5))
  

alpha_diversity_simpson_biogeography


alpha_diversity_Chao1_biogeography <- plot_richness(btphylo_atsacrifice, x="Biogeography", color = "Treatment", measures=c("Chao1"))+
  geom_boxplot()+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"))+
  labs(y = "Alpha Diversity - Chao1", x = "Biogeography", color = "Treatment Group")+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 0.5, hjust=0.5))
  

alpha_diversity_Chao1_biogeography

  
# Step 1: Estimate Fisher alpha diversity from the phyloseq object
fisher_diversity <- estimate_richness(btphylo_atsacrifice, measures = "Fisher")

# Step 2: Combine Fisher diversity with sample data
# Convert the Fisher diversity to a data frame
fisher_df <- as.data.frame(fisher_diversity)

# Ensure the row names (samples) are included in the data frame
fisher_df$Sample <- rownames(fisher_df)

# Merge with your metadata
combined_df <- fisher_df %>%
  left_join(metadata_all, by = "Sample")  # Adjust "SampleID" according to your metadata

# Set factor levels for Treatment to ensure "PBS" comes before "Bacteroides"
combined_df$Treatment <- factor(combined_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))

# Set factor levels for Biogeography in the desired order
combined_df$Biogeography <- factor(combined_df$Biogeography, 
                                            levels = c("SmallIntestine", "Cecum", "ProximalColon", "DistalColon"))



# Step 3: Create the alpha diversity plot for Fisher's measure across gut
alpha_fisher_across_gut <- ggplot(combined_df, 
                                   aes(x = Biogeography, y = Fisher, fill = Treatment)) +
  geom_boxplot(color = "black", alpha = 0.6) +  # Boxplot with borders and transparency
  geom_point(aes(color = Treatment, shape = Sex),  # Points mapped to Treatment and shape
             position = position_jitterdodge(dodge.width = 0.75),  # Jitter points for visibility
             alpha = 0.8, size = 2) +  # Points with defined aesthetics
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", 
                                "PBS" = "lightblue", 
                                "Bacteroides" = "orange")) +  # Fill colors for treatments
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", 
                                 "PBS" = "lightblue", 
                                 "Bacteroides" = "orange")) +  # Color mapping for points
  theme_minimal() +theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Biogeography",
       y = expression("Alpha Diversity - Fisher"),  # Y-axis label with expression
       fill = "Treatment") +  # Updated fill label
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),  # Rotate x-axis labels
        legend.position = "right")  # Position the legend

# Display the plot
print(alpha_fisher_across_gut)


ggsave("alpha_diversity_fisher_boxplot_acrossgut.pdf", plot = alpha_fisher_across_gut, device = "pdf", width = 5, height = 4)

#Run stats to see if there's a region that has more alpha diversity at baseline
# Subset the data for Baseline
acrossgut_baseline_fisher_df <- combined_df %>%
  filter(Treatment == "Baseline")

shapiro_acrossgut_baseline <- shapiro.test(acrossgut_baseline_fisher_df$Fisher)
shapiro_acrossgut_baseline

fisher_alpha_lm_acrossgut_baseline <- lm(Fisher ~ Biogeography+Sex+Biogeography*Sex+Microniche, data = acrossgut_baseline_fisher_df)
summary(fisher_alpha_lm_acrossgut_baseline)


#Run stats for alpha diversity along the gut. Run a lm for each region to see if there's a difference between PBS, 

# Subset the data for SmallIntestine
small_intestine_fisher_df <- combined_df %>%
  filter(Biogeography == "SmallIntestine")


small_intestine_pvb_fisher_df <- small_intestine_fisher_df %>% filter(Treatment %in% c("PBS", "Bacteroides"))


# Run the linear model on the subset
shapiro_acrossgut_smallintestine_fisher <- shapiro.test(small_intestine_fisher_df$Fisher)
shapiro_acrossgut_smallintestine_fisher

fisher_alpha_lm_small_intestine <- lm(Fisher ~ Treatment + Sex + Microniche+Treatment*Sex, data = small_intestine_fisher_df)
summary(fisher_alpha_lm_small_intestine)

fisher_alpha_lm_small_intestine_pvb <- lm(Fisher ~ Treatment + Sex + Microniche+Treatment*Sex, data = small_intestine_pvb_fisher_df)
summary(fisher_alpha_lm_small_intestine_pvb)

# Subset the data for Cecum
Cecum_fisher_df <- combined_df %>%
  filter(Biogeography == "Cecum")



cecum_pvb_fisher_df <- Cecum_fisher_df %>% filter(Treatment %in% c("PBS", "Bacteroides"))


Cecum_fisher_df$Treatment <- factor(Cecum_fisher_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))

# Run the linear model on the subset
shapiro_acrossgut_cecum_fisher <- shapiro.test(Cecum_fisher_df$Fisher)
shapiro_acrossgut_cecum_fisher

fisher_alpha_lm_cecum <- lm(log2(Fisher) ~ Treatment + Sex + Microniche + Treatment*Sex, data = Cecum_fisher_df)
summary(fisher_alpha_lm_cecum)

shapiro_acrossgut_cecum_fisher_pvb <- shapiro.test(cecum_pvb_fisher_df$Fisher)
shapiro_acrossgut_cecum_fisher_pvb

fisher_alpha_lm_cecum_pvb <- lm(Fisher ~ Treatment + Sex + Microniche + Treatment*Sex, data = cecum_pvb_fisher_df)
summary(fisher_alpha_lm_cecum_pvb)

# Subset the data for ProximalColon

ProximalColon_fisher_df <- combined_df %>%
  filter(Biogeography == "ProximalColon")

proximalcolon_pvb_fisher_df <- ProximalColon_fisher_df %>% filter(Treatment %in% c("PBS", "Bacteroides"))


ProximalColon_fisher_df$Treatment <- factor(ProximalColon_fisher_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))

ProximalColon_fisher_df$Treatment <- factor(ProximalColon_fisher_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("PBS", "Bacteroides", "Baseline"))


# Run the linear model on the subset
shapiro_acrossgut_proximalcolon_fisher <- shapiro.test(ProximalColon_fisher_df$Fisher)
shapiro_acrossgut_proximalcolon_fisher

fisher_alpha_lm_ProximalColon <- lm(log2(Fisher) ~ Treatment + Sex + Microniche+Treatment*Sex, data = ProximalColon_fisher_df)
summary(fisher_alpha_lm_ProximalColon)

shapiro_acrossgut_proximalcolon_fisher_pvb<- shapiro.test(proximalcolon_pvb_fisher_df$Fisher)
shapiro_acrossgut_proximalcolon_fisher_pvb

fisher_alpha_lm_ProximalColon_pvb <- lm(Fisher ~ Treatment + Sex + Microniche+Treatment*Sex, data = proximalcolon_pvb_fisher_df)
summary(fisher_alpha_lm_ProximalColon_pvb)


# Subset the data for DistalColon

DistalColon_fisher_df <- combined_df %>%
  filter(Biogeography == "DistalColon")

distalcolon_pvb_fisher_df <- DistalColon_fisher_df %>% filter(Treatment %in% c("PBS", "Bacteroides"))


DistalColon_fisher_df$Treatment <- factor(DistalColon_fisher_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))

DistalColon_fisher_df$Treatment <- factor(DistalColon_fisher_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("PBS", "Bacteroides", "Baseline"))


# Run the linear model on the subset
shapiro_acrossgut_distalcolon_fisher <- shapiro.test(DistalColon_fisher_df$Fisher)
shapiro_acrossgut_distalcolon_fisher

fisher_alpha_lm_DistalColon <- lm(log2(Fisher) ~ Treatment + Sex + Microniche+Treatment*Sex, data = DistalColon_fisher_df)
summary(fisher_alpha_lm_DistalColon)

shapiro_acrossgut_distalcolon_fisher_pvb <- shapiro.test(distalcolon_pvb_fisher_df$Fisher)
shapiro_acrossgut_distalcolon_fisher_pvb

fisher_alpha_lm_DistalColon_pvb <- lm(Fisher ~ Treatment + Sex + Microniche+Treatment*Sex, data = distalcolon_pvb_fisher_df)
summary(fisher_alpha_lm_DistalColon_pvb)


#Plot alpha diverisity for microniche
btphylo_atsacrifice_microniche <- subset_samples(btphylo_atsacrifice, !is.na(Microniche) & Microniche != "")

alpha_diversity_shannon_Microniche <- plot_richness(btphylo_atsacrifice_microniche, x="Microniche", color = "Treatment", measures=c("Shannon"))+
  geom_boxplot()+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"))+
  labs(y = "Alpha Diversity - Shannon", x = "Microniche", color = "Treatment Group")+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 0.5, hjust=0.5))
  

alpha_diversity_shannon_Microniche

alpha_diversity_Chao1_Microniche <- plot_richness(btphylo_atsacrifice_microniche, x="Microniche", color = "Treatment", measures=c("Chao1"))+
  geom_boxplot()+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"))+
  labs(y = "Alpha Diversity - Chao-1", x = "Microniche", color = "Treatment Group")+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 0.5, hjust=0.5))
  

alpha_diversity_Chao1_Microniche

#Plot alpha diversity between microniches
combined_df_filtered <- combined_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(Microniche))  # Keep only rows where Microniche is not NA


alpha_fisher_across_microniche <- ggplot(combined_df_filtered, 
                                   aes(x = Microniche, y = Fisher, fill = Treatment)) +
  geom_boxplot(color = "black", alpha = 0.6) +  # Boxplot with borders and transparency
  geom_point(aes(color = Treatment, shape = Sex),  # Points mapped to Treatment and shape
             position = position_jitterdodge(dodge.width = 0.75),  # Jitter points for visibility
             alpha = 0.8, size = 2) +  # Points with defined aesthetics
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", 
                                "PBS" = "lightblue", 
                                "Bacteroides" = "orange")) +  # Fill colors for treatments
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", 
                                 "PBS" = "lightblue", 
                                 "Bacteroides" = "orange")) +  # Color mapping for points
  theme_minimal() +theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Microniche",
       y = expression("Alpha Diversity - Fisher"),  # Y-axis label with expression
       fill = "Treatment") +  # Updated fill label
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),  # Rotate x-axis labels
        legend.position = "right")  # Position the legend

# Display the plot
print(alpha_fisher_across_microniche)

ggsave("alpha_diversity_fisher_boxplot_microniche.pdf", plot = alpha_fisher_across_microniche, device = "pdf", width = 5, height = 4)

#Run stats to see if there's a microniche that has more alpha diversity at baseline
# Subset the data for Baseline
microniche_baseline_fisher_df <- combined_df_filtered %>%
  filter(Treatment == "Baseline")

shapiro_microniche_baseline_fisher <- shapiro.test(microniche_baseline_fisher_df$Fisher)
shapiro_microniche_baseline_fisher

fisher_alpha_lm_microniche_baseline <- lm(Fisher ~ Microniche+Microniche*Sex+Biogeography+Microniche*Biogeography, data = microniche_baseline_fisher_df)
summary(fisher_alpha_lm_microniche_baseline)




# Subset the data for LuminalContents

luminalcontents_fisher_df <- combined_df_filtered %>%
  filter(Microniche == "LuminalContents")

luminalcontents_pvb_fisher_df <- luminalcontents_fisher_df %>% filter(Treatment %in% c("PBS", "Bacteroides"))


luminalcontents_fisher_df$Treatment <- factor(luminalcontents_fisher_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))

luminalcontents_fisher_df$Treatment <- factor(luminalcontents_fisher_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("PBS", "Bacteroides", "Baseline"))

# Run the linear model on the subset
shapiro_microniche_luminalcontents_fisher <- shapiro.test(luminalcontents_fisher_df$Fisher)
shapiro_microniche_luminalcontents_fisher

fisher_alpha_lm_luminalcontents <- lm(log2(Fisher) ~ Treatment + Sex +Treatment*Sex+Biogeography, data = luminalcontents_fisher_df)
summary(fisher_alpha_lm_luminalcontents)

shapiro_microniche_luminalcontents_fisher_pvb <- shapiro.test(luminalcontents_pvb_fisher_df$Fisher)
shapiro_microniche_luminalcontents_fisher_pvb

fisher_alpha_pvb_lm_luminalcontents <- lm(Fisher ~ Treatment + Sex +Treatment*Sex+Biogeography, data = luminalcontents_pvb_fisher_df)
summary(fisher_alpha_pvb_lm_luminalcontents)

# Subset the data for MucosalSurface

MucosalSurface_fisher_df <- combined_df_filtered %>%
  filter(Microniche == "MucosalSurface")

MucosalSurface_pvb_fisher_df <- MucosalSurface_fisher_df %>% filter(Treatment %in% c("PBS", "Bacteroides"))


MucosalSurface_fisher_df$Treatment <- factor(MucosalSurface_fisher_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))

MucosalSurface_fisher_df$Treatment <- factor(MucosalSurface_fisher_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("PBS", "Bacteroides", "Baseline"))

# Run the linear model on the subset
shapiro_microniche_mucosalsurface_fisher <- shapiro.test(MucosalSurface_fisher_df$Fisher)
shapiro_microniche_mucosalsurface_fisher

fisher_alpha_lm_MucosalSurface <- lm(log2(Fisher) ~ Treatment + Sex +Treatment*Sex + Biogeography, data = MucosalSurface_fisher_df)
summary(fisher_alpha_lm_MucosalSurface)

shapiro_microniche_mucosalsurface_fisher_pvb <- shapiro.test(MucosalSurface_pvb_fisher_df$Fisher)
shapiro_microniche_mucosalsurface_fisher_pvb

fisher_alpha_lm_MucosalSurface_pvb <- lm(Fisher ~ Treatment + Sex +Treatment*Sex+Biogeography, data = MucosalSurface_pvb_fisher_df)
summary(fisher_alpha_lm_MucosalSurface_pvb)

```


Plot the Pielou's Evenness and Observed Richness across gut
```{r}
# Step 1: Estimate Shannon alpha diversity from the phyloseq object
Shannon_diversity_atsacrifice <- estimate_richness(btphylo_atsacrifice, measures = "Shannon")
observed_diversity_atsacrifice <- estimate_richness(btphylo_atsacrifice, measures = "Observed")


# Step 2: Combine Shannon diversity with sample data
# Convert the Shannon diversity to a data frame
Shannon_atsacrifice_df <- as.data.frame(Shannon_diversity_atsacrifice)
observed_atsacrifice_df <- as.data.frame(observed_diversity_atsacrifice)


# Ensure the row names (samples) are included in the data frame
Shannon_atsacrifice_df$Sample <- rownames(Shannon_atsacrifice_df)

observed_atsacrifice_df$Sample <- rownames(observed_atsacrifice_df)
observed_atsacrifice_df$Sample <- gsub("X", "", observed_atsacrifice_df$Sample) #remove the x that is coming up for some reason


metadata_all$Sample <- rownames(metadata_all)

# Merge with your metadata
combined_atsacrifice_df <- Shannon_atsacrifice_df %>%
  left_join(metadata_all, by ="Sample")  # Adjust "SampleID" according to your metadata

# Merge with your metadata
combined_atsacrifice_df <- combined_atsacrifice_df %>%
  left_join(observed_atsacrifice_df, by ="Sample")  # Adjust "SampleID" according to your metadata

combined_atsacrifice_df$Pielou <- combined_atsacrifice_df$Shannon / log(combined_atsacrifice_df$Observed)

# Set factor levels for Treatment to ensure "PBS" comes before "Bacteroides"
combined_atsacrifice_df$Treatment <- factor(combined_atsacrifice_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))

# Set factor levels for Treatment to ensure "PBS" comes before "Bacteroides"
combined_atsacrifice_df$Biogeography <- factor(combined_atsacrifice_df$Biogeography, 
                                         levels = c("SmallIntestine", "Cecum", "ProximalColon", "DistalColon"))


combined_atsacrifice_filtered <- combined_atsacrifice_df %>%
  filter(Compiled_Timepoint != "Baseline")  # Keep only rows where Compiled_Timepoint is not "Baseline"



alpha_Observed_across_gut <- ggplot(combined_atsacrifice_filtered, 
                                   aes(x = Biogeography, y = Observed, fill = Treatment)) +
  geom_boxplot(color = "black", alpha = 0.6) +  # Boxplot with borders and transparency
  geom_point(aes(color = Treatment, shape = Sex),  # Points mapped to Treatment and shape
             position = position_jitterdodge(dodge.width = 0.75),  # Jitter points for visibility
             alpha = 0.8, size = 2) +  # Points with defined aesthetics
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", 
                                "PBS" = "lightblue", 
                                "Bacteroides" = "orange")) +  # Fill colors for treatments
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", 
                                 "PBS" = "lightblue", 
                                 "Bacteroides" = "orange")) +  # Color mapping for points
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(x = "Experiment Day",
       y = expression("Alpha Diversity - Observed"),  # Y-axis label with expression
       fill = "Treatment") +  # Updated fill label
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),  # Rotate x-axis labels
        legend.position = "right")  # Position the legend

# Display the plot
print(alpha_Observed_across_gut)


# Step 3: Create the alpha diversity plot for Shannon's measure across gut
alpha_Pielou_across_gut <- ggplot(combined_atsacrifice_filtered, 
                                   aes(x = Biogeography, y = Pielou, fill = Treatment)) +
  geom_boxplot(color = "black", alpha = 0.6) +  # Boxplot with borders and transparency
  geom_point(aes(color = Treatment, shape = Sex),  # Points mapped to Treatment and shape
             position = position_jitterdodge(dodge.width = 0.75),  # Jitter points for visibility
             alpha = 0.8, size = 2) +  # Points with defined aesthetics
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", 
                                "PBS" = "lightblue", 
                                "Bacteroides" = "orange")) +  # Fill colors for treatments
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", 
                                 "PBS" = "lightblue", 
                                 "Bacteroides" = "orange")) +  # Color mapping for points
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(x = "Biogeography",
       y = expression("Alpha Diversity - Pielou"),  # Y-axis label with expression
       fill = "Treatment") +  # Updated fill label
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),  # Rotate x-axis labels
        legend.position = "right")  # Position the legend

# Display the plot
print(alpha_Pielou_across_gut)

ggsave("alpha_Pielou_across_gut_boxplot.pdf", plot = alpha_Pielou_across_gut, device = "pdf", width = 6, height = 4)

ggsave("alpha_Observed_across_gut_boxplot.pdf", plot = alpha_Observed_across_gut, device = "pdf", width = 6, height = 4)

```
#Run stats on observed alpha diversity across biogeography
```{r}
#Run stats to see if there's a region that has more alpha diversity at baseline
# Subset the data for Baseline
acrossgut_baseline_Observed_df <- combined_atsacrifice_filtered %>%
  filter(Treatment == "Baseline")

shapiro_acrossgut_baseline_observed <- shapiro.test(acrossgut_baseline_Observed_df$Observed)
shapiro_acrossgut_baseline_observed

Observed_alpha_lm_acrossgut_baseline <- lm(Observed ~ Biogeography+Sex+Biogeography*Sex+Microniche, data = acrossgut_baseline_Observed_df)
summary(Observed_alpha_lm_acrossgut_baseline)


#Run stats for alpha diversity along the gut. Run a lm for each region to see if there's a difference between PBS, 

# Subset the data for SmallIntestine
small_intestine_Observed_df <- combined_atsacrifice_filtered %>%
  filter(Biogeography == "SmallIntestine")


small_intestine_pvb_Observed_df <- small_intestine_Observed_df %>% filter(Treatment %in% c("PBS", "Bacteroides"))


# Run the linear model on the subset
shapiro_acrossgut_smallintestine_Observed <- shapiro.test(small_intestine_Observed_df$Observed)
shapiro_acrossgut_smallintestine_Observed

Observed_alpha_lm_small_intestine <- lm(Observed ~ Treatment + Sex + Microniche+Treatment*Sex, data = small_intestine_Observed_df)
summary(Observed_alpha_lm_small_intestine)

shapiro_acrossgut_smallintestine_Observed_pvb <- shapiro.test(small_intestine_pvb_Observed_df$Observed)
shapiro_acrossgut_smallintestine_Observed_pvb

Observed_alpha_lm_small_intestine_pvb <- lm(Observed ~ Treatment + Sex + Microniche+Treatment*Sex, data = small_intestine_pvb_Observed_df)
summary(Observed_alpha_lm_small_intestine_pvb)

# Subset the data for Cecum
Cecum_Observed_df <- combined_atsacrifice_filtered %>%
  filter(Biogeography == "Cecum")



cecum_pvb_Observed_df <- Cecum_Observed_df %>% filter(Treatment %in% c("PBS", "Bacteroides"))


Cecum_Observed_df$Treatment <- factor(Cecum_Observed_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))

# Run the linear model on the subset
shapiro_acrossgut_cecum_Observed <- shapiro.test(Cecum_Observed_df$Observed)
shapiro_acrossgut_cecum_Observed

Observed_alpha_lm_cecum <- lm(log2(Observed) ~ Treatment + Sex + Microniche + Treatment*Sex, data = Cecum_Observed_df)
summary(Observed_alpha_lm_cecum)

shapiro_acrossgut_cecum_Observed_pvb <- shapiro.test(cecum_pvb_Observed_df$Observed)
shapiro_acrossgut_cecum_Observed_pvb

Observed_alpha_lm_cecum_pvb <- lm(Observed ~ Treatment + Sex + Microniche + Treatment*Sex, data = cecum_pvb_Observed_df)
summary(Observed_alpha_lm_cecum_pvb)

# Subset the data for ProximalColon

ProximalColon_Observed_df <- combined_atsacrifice_filtered %>%
  filter(Biogeography == "ProximalColon")

proximalcolon_pvb_Observed_df <- ProximalColon_Observed_df %>% filter(Treatment %in% c("PBS", "Bacteroides"))


ProximalColon_Observed_df$Treatment <- factor(ProximalColon_Observed_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))

ProximalColon_Observed_df$Treatment <- factor(ProximalColon_Observed_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("PBS", "Bacteroides", "Baseline"))


# Run the linear model on the subset
shapiro_acrossgut_proximalcolon_Observed <- shapiro.test(ProximalColon_Observed_df$Observed)
shapiro_acrossgut_proximalcolon_Observed

Observed_alpha_lm_ProximalColon <- lm(log2(Observed) ~ Treatment + Sex + Microniche+Treatment*Sex, data = ProximalColon_Observed_df)
summary(Observed_alpha_lm_ProximalColon)

shapiro_acrossgut_proximalcolon_Observed_pvb<- shapiro.test(proximalcolon_pvb_Observed_df$Observed)
shapiro_acrossgut_proximalcolon_Observed_pvb

Observed_alpha_lm_ProximalColon_pvb <- lm(log2(Observed) ~ Treatment + Sex + Microniche+Treatment*Sex, data = proximalcolon_pvb_Observed_df)
summary(Observed_alpha_lm_ProximalColon_pvb)


# Subset the data for DistalColon

DistalColon_Observed_df <- combined_atsacrifice_filtered %>%
  filter(Biogeography == "DistalColon")

distalcolon_pvb_Observed_df <- DistalColon_Observed_df %>% filter(Treatment %in% c("PBS", "Bacteroides"))


DistalColon_Observed_df$Treatment <- factor(DistalColon_Observed_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))

DistalColon_Observed_df$Treatment <- factor(DistalColon_Observed_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("PBS", "Bacteroides", "Baseline"))


# Run the linear model on the subset
shapiro_acrossgut_distalcolon_Observed <- shapiro.test(DistalColon_Observed_df$Observed)
shapiro_acrossgut_distalcolon_Observed

Observed_alpha_lm_DistalColon <- lm(log2(Observed) ~ Treatment + Sex + Microniche+Treatment*Sex, data = DistalColon_Observed_df)
summary(Observed_alpha_lm_DistalColon)

shapiro_acrossgut_distalcolon_Observed_pvb <- shapiro.test(distalcolon_pvb_Observed_df$Observed)
shapiro_acrossgut_distalcolon_Observed_pvb

Observed_alpha_lm_DistalColon_pvb <- lm(log2(Observed) ~ Treatment + Sex + Microniche+Treatment*Sex, data = distalcolon_pvb_Observed_df)
summary(Observed_alpha_lm_DistalColon_pvb)

```

#Run stats on Pielou alpha diversity across biogeography
```{r}
#Run stats to see if there's a region that has more alpha diversity at baseline
# Subset the data for Baseline
acrossgut_baseline_Pielou_df <- combined_atsacrifice_filtered %>%
  filter(Treatment == "Baseline")

shapiro_acrossgut_baseline_Pielou <- shapiro.test(acrossgut_baseline_Pielou_df$Pielou)
shapiro_acrossgut_baseline_Pielou

Pielou_alpha_lm_acrossgut_baseline <- lm(log2(Pielou) ~ Biogeography+Sex+Biogeography*Sex+Microniche, data = acrossgut_baseline_Pielou_df)
summary(Pielou_alpha_lm_acrossgut_baseline)


#Run stats for alpha diversity along the gut. Run a lm for each region to see if there's a difference between PBS, 

# Subset the data for SmallIntestine
small_intestine_Pielou_df <- combined_atsacrifice_filtered %>%
  filter(Biogeography == "SmallIntestine")


small_intestine_pvb_Pielou_df <- small_intestine_Pielou_df %>% filter(Treatment %in% c("PBS", "Bacteroides"))


# Run the linear model on the subset
shapiro_acrossgut_smallintestine_Pielou <- shapiro.test(small_intestine_Pielou_df$Pielou)
shapiro_acrossgut_smallintestine_Pielou

Pielou_alpha_lm_small_intestine <- lm(log2(Pielou) ~ Treatment + Sex + Microniche+Treatment*Sex, data = small_intestine_Pielou_df)
summary(Pielou_alpha_lm_small_intestine)

shapiro_acrossgut_smallintestine_Pielou_pvb <- shapiro.test(small_intestine_pvb_Pielou_df$Pielou)
shapiro_acrossgut_smallintestine_Pielou_pvb

Pielou_alpha_lm_small_intestine_pvb <- lm(Pielou ~ Treatment + Sex + Microniche+Treatment*Sex, data = small_intestine_pvb_Pielou_df)
summary(Pielou_alpha_lm_small_intestine_pvb)

# Subset the data for Cecum
Cecum_Pielou_df <- combined_atsacrifice_filtered %>%
  filter(Biogeography == "Cecum")



cecum_pvb_Pielou_df <- Cecum_Pielou_df %>% filter(Treatment %in% c("PBS", "Bacteroides"))


Cecum_Pielou_df$Treatment <- factor(Cecum_Pielou_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))

# Run the linear model on the subset
shapiro_acrossgut_cecum_Pielou <- shapiro.test(Cecum_Pielou_df$Pielou)
shapiro_acrossgut_cecum_Pielou

Pielou_alpha_lm_cecum <- lm(Pielou ~ Treatment + Sex + Microniche + Treatment*Sex, data = Cecum_Pielou_df)
summary(Pielou_alpha_lm_cecum)

shapiro_acrossgut_cecum_Pielou_pvb <- shapiro.test(cecum_pvb_Pielou_df$Pielou)
shapiro_acrossgut_cecum_Pielou_pvb

Pielou_alpha_lm_cecum_pvb <- lm(Pielou ~ Treatment + Sex + Microniche + Treatment*Sex, data = cecum_pvb_Pielou_df)
summary(Pielou_alpha_lm_cecum_pvb)

# Subset the data for ProximalColon

ProximalColon_Pielou_df <- combined_atsacrifice_filtered %>%
  filter(Biogeography == "ProximalColon")

proximalcolon_pvb_Pielou_df <- ProximalColon_Pielou_df %>% filter(Treatment %in% c("PBS", "Bacteroides"))


ProximalColon_Pielou_df$Treatment <- factor(ProximalColon_Pielou_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))

ProximalColon_Pielou_df$Treatment <- factor(ProximalColon_Pielou_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("PBS", "Bacteroides", "Baseline"))


# Run the linear model on the subset
shapiro_acrossgut_proximalcolon_Pielou <- shapiro.test(ProximalColon_Pielou_df$Pielou)
shapiro_acrossgut_proximalcolon_Pielou

Pielou_alpha_lm_ProximalColon <- lm(log2(Pielou) ~ Treatment + Sex + Microniche+Treatment*Sex, data = ProximalColon_Pielou_df)
summary(Pielou_alpha_lm_ProximalColon)

shapiro_acrossgut_proximalcolon_Pielou_pvb<- shapiro.test(proximalcolon_pvb_Pielou_df$Pielou)
shapiro_acrossgut_proximalcolon_Pielou_pvb

Pielou_alpha_lm_ProximalColon_pvb <- lm(log2(Pielou) ~ Treatment + Sex + Microniche+Treatment*Sex, data = proximalcolon_pvb_Pielou_df)
summary(Pielou_alpha_lm_ProximalColon_pvb)


# Subset the data for DistalColon

DistalColon_Pielou_df <- combined_atsacrifice_filtered %>%
  filter(Biogeography == "DistalColon")

distalcolon_pvb_Pielou_df <- DistalColon_Pielou_df %>% filter(Treatment %in% c("PBS", "Bacteroides"))


DistalColon_Pielou_df$Treatment <- factor(DistalColon_Pielou_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))

DistalColon_Pielou_df$Treatment <- factor(DistalColon_Pielou_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("PBS", "Bacteroides", "Baseline"))


# Run the linear model on the subset
shapiro_acrossgut_distalcolon_Pielou <- shapiro.test(DistalColon_Pielou_df$Pielou)
shapiro_acrossgut_distalcolon_Pielou

Pielou_alpha_lm_DistalColon <- lm(Pielou ~ Treatment + Sex + Microniche+Treatment*Sex, data = DistalColon_Pielou_df)
summary(Pielou_alpha_lm_DistalColon)

shapiro_acrossgut_distalcolon_Pielou_pvb <- shapiro.test(distalcolon_pvb_Pielou_df$Pielou)
shapiro_acrossgut_distalcolon_Pielou_pvb

Pielou_alpha_lm_DistalColon_pvb <- lm(Pielou ~ Treatment + Sex + Microniche+Treatment*Sex, data = distalcolon_pvb_Pielou_df)
summary(Pielou_alpha_lm_DistalColon_pvb)

```

Plot the Pielou's Evenness and Observed Richness across microniche

```{r}

combined_atsacrifice_filtered_microniche <- combined_atsacrifice_filtered %>%
  filter(!is.na(Microniche))  # Keep only rows where Microniche is not NA


alpha_Observed_across_microniche <- ggplot(combined_atsacrifice_filtered_microniche, 
                                   aes(x = Microniche, y = Observed, fill = Treatment)) +
  geom_boxplot(color = "black", alpha = 0.6) +  # Boxplot with borders and transparency
  geom_point(aes(color = Treatment, shape = Sex),  # Points mapped to Treatment and shape
             position = position_jitterdodge(dodge.width = 0.75),  # Jitter points for visibility
             alpha = 0.8, size = 2) +  # Points with defined aesthetics
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", 
                                "PBS" = "lightblue", 
                                "Bacteroides" = "orange")) +  # Fill colors for treatments
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", 
                                 "PBS" = "lightblue", 
                                 "Bacteroides" = "orange")) +  # Color mapping for points
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(x = "Microniche",
       y = expression("Alpha Diversity - Observed"),  # Y-axis label with expression
       fill = "Treatment") +  # Updated fill label
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),  # Rotate x-axis labels
        legend.position = "right")  # Position the legend

alpha_Observed_across_microniche


alpha_Pielou_across_microniche <- ggplot(combined_atsacrifice_filtered_microniche, 
                                   aes(x = Microniche, y = Pielou, fill = Treatment)) +
  geom_boxplot(color = "black", alpha = 0.6) +  # Boxplot with borders and transparency
  geom_point(aes(color = Treatment, shape = Sex),  # Points mapped to Treatment and shape
             position = position_jitterdodge(dodge.width = 0.75),  # Jitter points for visibility
             alpha = 0.8, size = 2) +  # Points with defined aesthetics
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", 
                                "PBS" = "lightblue", 
                                "Bacteroides" = "orange")) +  # Fill colors for treatments
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", 
                                 "PBS" = "lightblue", 
                                 "Bacteroides" = "orange")) +  # Color mapping for points
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(x = "Microniche",
       y = expression("Alpha Diversity - Pielou"),  # Y-axis label with expression
       fill = "Treatment") +  # Updated fill label
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),  # Rotate x-axis labels
        legend.position = "right")  # Position the legend

alpha_Pielou_across_microniche

ggsave("alpha_Pielou_across__microniche_boxplot.pdf", plot = alpha_Pielou_across_microniche, device = "pdf", width = 6, height = 4)

ggsave("alpha_Observed_across_microniche_boxplot.pdf", plot = alpha_Observed_across_microniche, device = "pdf", width = 6, height = 4)

```

#Run stats on Observed alpha diversity across microniches
```{r}
#Run stats to see if there's a microniche that has more alpha diversity at baseline
# Subset the data for Baseline
microniche_baseline_Observed_df <- combined_atsacrifice_filtered_microniche %>%
  filter(Treatment == "Baseline")

shapiro_microniche_baseline_Observed <- shapiro.test(microniche_baseline_Observed_df$Observed)
shapiro_microniche_baseline_Observed

Observed_alpha_lm_microniche_baseline <- lm(log2(Observed) ~ Microniche+Microniche*Sex+Biogeography+Microniche*Biogeography, data = microniche_baseline_Observed_df)
summary(Observed_alpha_lm_microniche_baseline)




# Subset the data for LuminalContents

luminalcontents_Observed_df <- combined_atsacrifice_filtered_microniche %>%
  filter(Microniche == "LuminalContents")

luminalcontents_pvb_Observed_df <- luminalcontents_Observed_df %>% filter(Treatment %in% c("PBS", "Bacteroides"))


luminalcontents_Observed_df$Treatment <- factor(luminalcontents_Observed_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))

luminalcontents_Observed_df$Treatment <- factor(luminalcontents_Observed_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("PBS", "Bacteroides", "Baseline"))

# Run the linear model on the subset
shapiro_microniche_luminalcontents_Observed <- shapiro.test(luminalcontents_Observed_df$Observed)
shapiro_microniche_luminalcontents_Observed

Observed_alpha_lm_luminalcontents <- lm(log2(Observed) ~ Treatment + Sex +Treatment*Sex+Biogeography, data = luminalcontents_Observed_df)
summary(Observed_alpha_lm_luminalcontents)

shapiro_microniche_luminalcontents_Observed_pvb <- shapiro.test(luminalcontents_pvb_Observed_df$Observed)
shapiro_microniche_luminalcontents_Observed_pvb

Observed_alpha_pvb_lm_luminalcontents <- lm(Observed ~ Treatment + Sex +Treatment*Sex+Biogeography, data = luminalcontents_pvb_Observed_df)
summary(Observed_alpha_pvb_lm_luminalcontents)

# Subset the data for MucosalSurface

MucosalSurface_Observed_df <- combined_atsacrifice_filtered_microniche %>%
  filter(Microniche == "MucosalSurface")

MucosalSurface_pvb_Observed_df <- MucosalSurface_Observed_df %>% filter(Treatment %in% c("PBS", "Bacteroides"))


MucosalSurface_Observed_df$Treatment <- factor(MucosalSurface_Observed_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))

MucosalSurface_Observed_df$Treatment <- factor(MucosalSurface_Observed_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("PBS", "Bacteroides", "Baseline"))

# Run the linear model on the subset
shapiro_microniche_mucosalsurface_Observed <- shapiro.test(MucosalSurface_Observed_df$Observed)
shapiro_microniche_mucosalsurface_Observed

Observed_alpha_lm_MucosalSurface <- lm(log2(Observed) ~ Treatment + Sex +Treatment*Sex + Biogeography, data = MucosalSurface_Observed_df)
summary(Observed_alpha_lm_MucosalSurface)

shapiro_microniche_mucosalsurface_Observed_pvb <- shapiro.test(MucosalSurface_pvb_Observed_df$Observed)
shapiro_microniche_mucosalsurface_Observed_pvb

Observed_alpha_lm_MucosalSurface_pvb <- lm(Observed ~ Treatment + Sex +Treatment*Sex+Biogeography, data = MucosalSurface_pvb_Observed_df)
summary(Observed_alpha_lm_MucosalSurface_pvb)

```

#Run stats on Pielou alpha diversity across microniches
```{r}
#Run stats to see if there's a microniche that has more alpha diversity at baseline
# Subset the data for Baseline
microniche_baseline_Pielou_df <- combined_atsacrifice_filtered_microniche %>%
  filter(Treatment == "Baseline")

shapiro_microniche_baseline_Pielou <- shapiro.test(microniche_baseline_Pielou_df$Pielou)
shapiro_microniche_baseline_Pielou

Pielou_alpha_lm_microniche_baseline <- lm(log2(Pielou) ~ Microniche+Microniche*Sex+Biogeography+Microniche*Biogeography, data = microniche_baseline_Pielou_df)
summary(Pielou_alpha_lm_microniche_baseline)




# Subset the data for LuminalContents

luminalcontents_Pielou_df <- combined_atsacrifice_filtered_microniche %>%
  filter(Microniche == "LuminalContents")

luminalcontents_pvb_Pielou_df <- luminalcontents_Pielou_df %>% filter(Treatment %in% c("PBS", "Bacteroides"))


luminalcontents_Pielou_df$Treatment <- factor(luminalcontents_Pielou_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))

luminalcontents_Pielou_df$Treatment <- factor(luminalcontents_Pielou_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("PBS", "Bacteroides", "Baseline"))

# Run the linear model on the subset
shapiro_microniche_luminalcontents_Pielou <- shapiro.test(luminalcontents_Pielou_df$Pielou)
shapiro_microniche_luminalcontents_Pielou

Pielou_alpha_lm_luminalcontents <- lm(log2(Pielou) ~ Treatment + Sex +Treatment*Sex+Biogeography, data = luminalcontents_Pielou_df)
summary(Pielou_alpha_lm_luminalcontents)

shapiro_microniche_luminalcontents_Pielou_pvb <- shapiro.test(luminalcontents_pvb_Pielou_df$Pielou)
shapiro_microniche_luminalcontents_Pielou_pvb

Pielou_alpha_pvb_lm_luminalcontents <- lm(Pielou ~ Treatment + Sex +Treatment*Sex+Biogeography, data = luminalcontents_pvb_Pielou_df)
summary(Pielou_alpha_pvb_lm_luminalcontents)

# Subset the data for MucosalSurface

MucosalSurface_Pielou_df <- combined_atsacrifice_filtered_microniche %>%
  filter(Microniche == "MucosalSurface")

MucosalSurface_pvb_Pielou_df <- MucosalSurface_Pielou_df %>% filter(Treatment %in% c("PBS", "Bacteroides"))


MucosalSurface_Pielou_df$Treatment <- factor(MucosalSurface_Pielou_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))

MucosalSurface_Pielou_df$Treatment <- factor(MucosalSurface_Pielou_df$Treatment, 
                                         levels = c("PBS", "Bacteroides", "Baseline"))

# Run the linear model on the subset
shapiro_microniche_mucosalsurface_Pielou <- shapiro.test(MucosalSurface_Pielou_df$Pielou)
shapiro_microniche_mucosalsurface_Pielou

Pielou_alpha_lm_MucosalSurface <- lm(Pielou ~ Treatment + Sex +Treatment*Sex + Biogeography, data = MucosalSurface_Pielou_df)
summary(Pielou_alpha_lm_MucosalSurface)

shapiro_microniche_mucosalsurface_Pielou_pvb <- shapiro.test(MucosalSurface_pvb_Pielou_df$Pielou)
shapiro_microniche_mucosalsurface_Pielou_pvb

Pielou_alpha_lm_MucosalSurface_pvb <- lm(Pielou ~ Treatment + Sex +Treatment*Sex+Biogeography, data = MucosalSurface_pvb_Pielou_df)
summary(Pielou_alpha_lm_MucosalSurface_pvb)

```



```

```{r}
#Plot the results with points colored by the Treatment group.
PCoA_plot_all <- plot_ordination(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed, ordinate_PCoA_bray, color = "Treatment",shape="Sex")+
  stat_ellipse(aes(group=Treatment)) + #adds an ellipse to a scatter plot to help visualize the distribution of points in the plot. The ellipse is drawn using a confidence interval, which can be specified using the level argument. By default, stat_ellipse() uses a 95% confidence interval.
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange")) +  # Corrected scale_color_manual
   facet_wrap(~Microniche, ncol=3)

PCoA_plot_all

# Save the PCoA plot as a PDF
ggsave("PCoA_plot_Microniches.pdf", plot = PCoA_plot_all, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)



```


#Run stats to see if Treatment has a significant effect on beta diversity in each biogeography, you can subset the data to only include samples from the small intestine and then run the PERMANOVA analysis. Here is the code that will do this:
```{r}


#Subset phyloseq object to only have the data from Small Intestine
btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_smallintestine <- subset_samples(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_clean, 
                                                                       Biogeography %in% "SmallIntestine")

#Calculate Bray-Curtis dissimilarity distance matrix
distance_bray_smallintestine = phyloseq::distance(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_smallintestine, method = "bray")

#Perform a principal coordinate analysis (PCoA) on the distance matrix
smallintestine_ordinate_PCoA_bray = ordinate(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_smallintestine, 
                                             method = "PCoA", 
                                             distance = distance_bray_smallintestine)


PCoA_plot_smallintestine <- plot_ordination(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_smallintestine, smallintestine_ordinate_PCoA_bray, color = "Treatment")+
  theme_bw()+
  stat_ellipse() #adds an ellipse to a scatter plot to help visualize the distribution of points in the plot. The ellipse is drawn using a confidence interval, which can be specified using the level argument. By default, stat_ellipse() uses a 95% confidence interval.
PCoA_plot_smallintestine+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"))

ord_smallintestine_df <- data.frame(sample_data(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_smallintestine))
ord_smallintestine_df$SeqCoastOrder_Cage_Mouse <- interaction(ord_smallintestine_df$SeqCoastOrder, ord_smallintestine_df$Cage, ord_smallintestine_df$Mouse, drop = TRUE)
ord_smallintestine_df$SeqCoastOrder_Cage <- interaction(ord_smallintestine_df$SeqCoastOrder, ord_smallintestine_df$Cage, drop = TRUE)

# Run PERMANOVA using adonis2
smallintestine_perm_results <- adonis2(distance_bray_smallintestine ~ Treatment + Sex + Treatment*Sex + Microniche + SeqCoastOrder_Cage_Mouse+SeqCoastOrder:Cage, 
                                       data = ord_smallintestine_df, 
                                       method="bray", 
                                       permutations = 999,
                                       by="terms")
 

smallintestine_perm_results



# Run pairwise Adonis
smallintestine_pairwise_results <- pairwise.adonis(
  x = distance_bray_smallintestine,                  # Bray-Curtis distance matrix
  factors = ord_smallintestine_df$Treatment,         # The grouping factor (Treatment)
  sim.function = "vegdist",                           # Use vegan distance function
  sim.method = "bray",                               # Bray-Curtis method
  p.adjust.m = "bonferroni",                         # Adjust p-values using Bonferroni correction
  perm = 999                                         # Number of permutations
)

# Print the results
print(smallintestine_pairwise_results)

```
#run stats on beta diversity in Cecum
```{r}
# Subset phyloseq object to only have the data from Cecum
btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_cecum <- subset_samples(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed, 
                                                              Biogeography %in% "Cecum")

# Calculate Bray-Curtis dissimilarity distance matrix for Cecum
distance_bray_cecum = phyloseq::distance(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_cecum, method = "bray")

# Perform a principal coordinate analysis (PCoA) on the distance matrix for Cecum
cecum_ordinate_PCoA_bray = ordinate(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_cecum, 
                                     method = "PCoA", 
                                     distance = distance_bray_cecum)

# Create PCoA plot for Cecum
PCoA_plot_cecum <- plot_ordination(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_cecum, cecum_ordinate_PCoA_bray, color = "Treatment") +
  theme_bw() +
  stat_ellipse() # adds an ellipse to the scatter plot for confidence intervals
PCoA_plot_cecum +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"))

# Convert sample data to a dataframe
ord_cecum_df <- data.frame(sample_data(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_cecum))
ord_cecum_df$SeqCoastOrder_Cage_Mouse <- interaction(ord_cecum_df$SeqCoastOrder, ord_cecum_df$Cage, ord_cecum_df$Mouse, drop = TRUE)
ord_cecum_df$SeqCoastOrder_Cage <- interaction(ord_cecum_df$SeqCoastOrder, ord_cecum_df$Cage, drop = TRUE)

# Run PERMANOVA using adonis2 for Cecum
cecum_perm_results <- adonis2(distance_bray_cecum ~ Treatment + Sex + Treatment*Sex+Microniche+SeqCoastOrder_Cage+SeqCoastOrder_Cage_Mouse, 
                              data = ord_cecum_df,
                              method="bray",
                              permutations = 999,
                              by="terms")
 

cecum_perm_results

# Run pairwise Adonis for Cecum
cecum_pairwise_results <- pairwise.adonis(
  x = distance_bray_cecum,                  # Bray-Curtis distance matrix
  factors = ord_cecum_df$Treatment,         # The grouping factor (Treatment)
  sim.function = "vegdist",                  # Use vegan distance function
  sim.method = "bray",                      # Bray-Curtis method
  p.adjust.m = "bonferroni",                # Adjust p-values using Bonferroni correction
  perm = 999                                # Number of permutations
)

# Print the results of pairwise Adonis for Cecum
print(cecum_pairwise_results)



```

#now run stats for beta diversity in proximal colon
```{r}
# Subset phyloseq object to only have the data from Proximal Colon
btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_proximalcolon <- subset_samples(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed, 
                                                                       Biogeography %in% "ProximalColon")

# Calculate Bray-Curtis dissimilarity distance matrix for Proximal Colon
distance_bray_proximalcolon = phyloseq::distance(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_proximalcolon, method = "bray")

# Perform a principal coordinate analysis (PCoA) on the distance matrix for Proximal Colon
proximalcolon_ordinate_PCoA_bray = ordinate(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_proximalcolon, 
                                            method = "PCoA", 
                                            distance = distance_bray_proximalcolon)

# Create PCoA plot for Proximal Colon
PCoA_plot_proximalcolon <- plot_ordination(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_proximalcolon, proximalcolon_ordinate_PCoA_bray, color = "Treatment") +
  theme_bw() +
  stat_ellipse() # adds an ellipse to the scatter plot for confidence intervals
PCoA_plot_proximalcolon +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"))

# Convert sample data to a dataframe
ord_proximalcolon_df <- data.frame(sample_data(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_proximalcolon))
ord_proximalcolon_df$SeqCoastOrder_Cage_Mouse <- interaction(ord_proximalcolon_df$SeqCoastOrder, ord_proximalcolon_df$Cage, ord_proximalcolon_df$Mouse, drop = TRUE)
ord_proximalcolon_df$SeqCoastOrder_Cage <- interaction(ord_proximalcolon_df$SeqCoastOrder, ord_proximalcolon_df$Cage, drop = TRUE)


# Run PERMANOVA using adonis2 for Proximal Colon
proximalcolon_perm_results <- adonis2(distance_bray_proximalcolon ~ Treatment + Sex + Treatment*Sex+Microniche+SeqCoastOrder_Cage+SeqCoastOrder_Cage_Mouse, 
                                      data = ord_proximalcolon_df, 
                                      permutations = 999,
                                      by="terms")
 

proximalcolon_perm_results

# Run pairwise Adonis for Proximal Colon
proximalcolon_pairwise_results <- pairwise.adonis(
  x = distance_bray_proximalcolon,                  # Bray-Curtis distance matrix
  factors = ord_proximalcolon_df$Treatment,         # The grouping factor (Treatment)
  sim.function = "vegdist",                         # Use vegan distance function
  sim.method = "bray",                              # Bray-Curtis method
  p.adjust.m = "bonferroni",                        # Adjust p-values using Bonferroni correction
  perm = 999                                        # Number of permutations
)

# Print the results of pairwise Adonis for Proximal Colon
print(proximalcolon_pairwise_results)



```
#Now stats for beta diversity in distal colon
```{r}
# Subset phyloseq object to only have the data from Distal Colon
btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_distalcolon <- subset_samples(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed, 
                                                                       Biogeography %in% "DistalColon")

# Calculate Bray-Curtis dissimilarity distance matrix for Distal Colon
distance_bray_distalcolon = phyloseq::distance(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_distalcolon, method = "bray")

# Perform a principal coordinate analysis (PCoA) on the distance matrix for Distal Colon
distalcolon_ordinate_PCoA_bray = ordinate(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_distalcolon, 
                                           method = "PCoA", 
                                           distance = distance_bray_distalcolon)

# Create PCoA plot for Distal Colon
PCoA_plot_distalcolon <- plot_ordination(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_distalcolon, distalcolon_ordinate_PCoA_bray, color = "Treatment") +
  theme_bw() +
  stat_ellipse() # adds an ellipse to the scatter plot for confidence intervals
PCoA_plot_distalcolon +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"))

# Convert sample data to a dataframe
ord_distalcolon_df <- data.frame(sample_data(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_distalcolon))
ord_distalcolon_df$SeqCoastOrder_Cage_Mouse <- interaction(ord_distalcolon_df$SeqCoastOrder, ord_distalcolon_df$Cage, ord_distalcolon_df$Mouse, drop = TRUE)
ord_distalcolon_df$SeqCoastOrder_Cage <- interaction(ord_distalcolon_df$SeqCoastOrder, ord_distalcolon_df$Cage, drop = TRUE)


# Run PERMANOVA using adonis2 for Distal Colon
distalcolon_perm_results <- adonis2(distance_bray_distalcolon ~ Treatment + Sex + Treatment*Sex+Microniche+SeqCoastOrder_Cage+SeqCoastOrder_Cage_Mouse, 
                                    data = ord_distalcolon_df, 
                                    permutations = 999,
                                      by="terms")
 

distalcolon_perm_results

# Run pairwise Adonis for Distal Colon
distalcolon_pairwise_results <- pairwise.adonis(
  x = distance_bray_distalcolon,                  # Bray-Curtis distance matrix
  factors = ord_distalcolon_df$Treatment,         # The grouping factor (Treatment)
  sim.function = "vegdist",                        # Use vegan distance function
  sim.method = "bray",                             # Bray-Curtis method
  p.adjust.m = "bonferroni",                       # Adjust p-values using Bonferroni correction
  perm = 999                                       # Number of permutations
)

# Print the results of pairwise Adonis for Distal Colon
print(distalcolon_pairwise_results)



```
#Mucusal Surface beta diversity pairwise adonis
```{r}
# Subset phyloseq object to only have the data from Mucosal Surface
btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_mucosal <- subset_samples(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed, 
                                                                Microniche %in% "MucosalSurface")

# Calculate Bray-Curtis dissimilarity distance matrix for Mucosal Surface
distance_bray_mucosal = phyloseq::distance(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_mucosal, method = "bray")

# Perform a principal coordinate analysis (PCoA) on the distance matrix for Mucosal Surface
mucosal_ordinate_PCoA_bray = ordinate(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_mucosal, 
                                       method = "PCoA", 
                                       distance = distance_bray_mucosal)

# Create PCoA plot for Mucosal Surface
PCoA_plot_mucosal <- plot_ordination(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_mucosal, mucosal_ordinate_PCoA_bray, color = "Treatment") +
  theme_bw() +
  stat_ellipse() # adds an ellipse to the scatter plot for confidence intervals
PCoA_plot_mucosal +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"))

# Convert sample data to a dataframe
ord_mucosal_df <- data.frame(sample_data(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_mucosal))
ord_mucosal_df$SeqCoastOrder_Cage_Mouse <- interaction(ord_mucosal_df$SeqCoastOrder, ord_mucosal_df$Cage, ord_mucosal_df$Mouse, drop = TRUE)
ord_mucosal_df$SeqCoastOrder_Cage <- interaction(ord_mucosal_df$SeqCoastOrder, ord_mucosal_df$Cage, drop = TRUE)



# Run PERMANOVA using adonis2 for Mucosal Surface
mucosal_perm_results <- adonis2(distance_bray_mucosal ~ Treatment + Sex + Biogeography + Treatment*Sex+SeqCoastOrder_Cage+SeqCoastOrder_Cage_Mouse,
                                data = ord_mucosal_df, 
                                permutations = 999,
                                by="terms")

# Print the results of PERMANOVA
print(mucosal_perm_results)

# Run pairwise Adonis for Mucosal Surface
mucosal_pairwise_results <- pairwise.adonis(
  x = distance_bray_mucosal,                        # Bray-Curtis distance matrix
  factors = ord_mucosal_df$Treatment,               # The grouping factor (Treatment)
  sim.function = "vegdist",                          # Use vegan distance function
  sim.method = "bray",                               # Bray-Curtis method
  p.adjust.m = "bonferroni",                         # Adjust p-values using Bonferroni correction
  perm = 999                                         # Number of permutations
)

# Print the results of pairwise Adonis for Mucosal Surface
print(mucosal_pairwise_results)




```
Luminal Contents beta diversity pairwise adonis
```{r}

# Subset phyloseq object to only have the data from Luminal Contents
btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_luminal <- subset_samples(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed, 
                                                                Microniche %in% "LuminalContents")

# Calculate Bray-Curtis dissimilarity distance matrix for Luminal Contents
distance_bray_luminal = phyloseq::distance(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_luminal, method = "bray")

# Perform a principal coordinate analysis (PCoA) on the distance matrix for Luminal Contents
luminal_ordinate_PCoA_bray = ordinate(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_luminal, 
                                       method = "PCoA", 
                                       distance = distance_bray_luminal)

# Create PCoA plot for Luminal Contents
PCoA_plot_luminal <- plot_ordination(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_luminal, luminal_ordinate_PCoA_bray, color = "Treatment") +
  theme_bw() +
  stat_ellipse() # adds an ellipse to the scatter plot for confidence intervals
PCoA_plot_luminal +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"))

# Convert sample data to a dataframe
ord_luminal_df <- data.frame(sample_data(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_luminal))
ord_luminal_df$SeqCoastOrder_Cage_Mouse <- interaction(ord_luminal_df$SeqCoastOrder, ord_luminal_df$Cage, ord_luminal_df$Mouse, drop = TRUE)
ord_luminal_df$SeqCoastOrder_Cage <- interaction(ord_luminal_df$SeqCoastOrder, ord_luminal_df$Cage, drop = TRUE)



# Run PERMANOVA using adonis2 for Luminal Contents
luminal_perm_results <- adonis2(distance_bray_luminal ~ Treatment + Sex + Biogeography + Treatment*Sex+SeqCoastOrder_Cage+SeqCoastOrder_Cage_Mouse, 
                                data = ord_luminal_df, 
                                permutations = 999,
                                by="terms")

# Print the results of PERMANOVA
print(luminal_perm_results)

# Run pairwise Adonis for Luminal Contents
luminal_pairwise_results <- pairwise.adonis(
  x = distance_bray_luminal,                        # Bray-Curtis distance matrix
  factors = ord_luminal_df$Treatment,               # The grouping factor (Treatment)
  sim.function = "vegdist",                          # Use vegan distance function
  sim.method = "bray",                               # Bray-Curtis method
  p.adjust.m = "bonferroni",                         # Adjust p-values using Bonferroni correction
  perm = 999                                         # Number of permutations
)

# Print the results of pairwise Adonis for Luminal Contents
print(luminal_pairwise_results)


```

Stool at sacrifice beta diversity pairwise adonis
```{r}

# Subset phyloseq object to only have the data from stool Contents
btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_stool <- subset_samples(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed, 
                                                                Microniche %in% "stool")

# Calculate Bray-Curtis dissimilarity distance matrix for stool Contents
distance_bray_stool = phyloseq::distance(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_stool, method = "bray")

# Perform a principal coordinate analysis (PCoA) on the distance matrix for stool Contents
stool_ordinate_PCoA_bray = ordinate(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_stool, 
                                       method = "PCoA", 
                                       distance = distance_bray_stool)

# Create PCoA plot for stool Contents
PCoA_plot_stool <- plot_ordination(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_stool, stool_ordinate_PCoA_bray, color = "Treatment", shape = "Sex") +
  theme_bw() +
  stat_ellipse(aes(group=Treatment)) # adds an ellipse to the scatter plot for confidence intervals
PCoA_plot_stool +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"))

# Convert sample data to a dataframe
ord_stool_df <- data.frame(sample_data(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_stool))
ord_stool_df$SeqCoastOrder_Cage_Mouse <- interaction(ord_stool_df$SeqCoastOrder, ord_stool_df$Cage, ord_stool_df$Mouse, drop = TRUE)
ord_stool_df$SeqCoastOrder_Cage <- interaction(ord_stool_df$SeqCoastOrder, ord_stool_df$Cage, drop = TRUE)


# Run PERMANOVA using adonis2 for stool Contents
stool_perm_results <- adonis2(distance_bray_stool ~ Treatment + Sex+Treatment*Sex+SeqCoastOrder_Cage+Seq, 
                              data = ord_stool_df, 
                              permutations = 999,
                                by="terms")


# Print the results of PERMANOVA
print(stool_perm_results)

# Run pairwise Adonis for stool Contents
stool_pairwise_results <- pairwise.adonis(
  x = distance_bray_stool,                        # Bray-Curtis distance matrix
  factors = ord_stool_df$Treatment,               # The grouping factor (Treatment)
  sim.function = "vegdist",                          # Use vegan distance function
  sim.method = "bray",                               # Bray-Curtis method
  p.adjust.m = "bonferroni",                         # Adjust p-values using Bonferroni correction
  perm = 999                                         # Number of permutations
)

# Print the results of pairwise Adonis for stool Contents
print(stool_pairwise_results)


```


```{r}
#######################Baseline########################
#Subset phyloseq object to only have the data from Baseline
btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_baseline <- subset_samples(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed, 
                                                               Treatment == c( "Baseline"))

#Calculate Bray-Curtis dissimilarity distance matrix
distance_bray_baseline = phyloseq::distance(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_baseline, method = "bray")

#Perform a principal coordinate analysis (PCoA) on the distance matrix
baseline_ordinate_PCoA_bray = ordinate(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_baseline, method = "PCoA", distance = "distance_bray_baseline")

PCoA_plot_baseline <- plot_ordination(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_baseline, baseline_ordinate_PCoA_bray, shape = "Sex", color = "Microniche")+
  theme_bw()+
  stat_ellipse()+ #adds an ellipse to a scatter plot to help visualize the distribution of points in the plot. The ellipse is drawn using a confidence interval, which can be specified using the level argument. By default, stat_ellipse() uses a 95% confidence interval.
   scale_color_manual(values = pastel_colors)  # Apply pastel colors

ord_Baseline_df <- data.frame(sample_data(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_baseline))

# Run PERMANOVA using adonis2
Baseline_perm_results <- adonis2(distance_bray_baseline ~ Microniche + Sex, data = ord_Baseline_df, permutations = 999)

Baseline_perm_results


#######################PBS########################
#Subset phyloseq object to only have the data from PBS
btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_PBS <- subset_samples(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed, 
                                                               Treatment == c( "PBS"))

#Calculate Bray-Curtis dissimilarity distance matrix
distance_bray_PBS = phyloseq::distance(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_PBS, method = "bray")

#Perform a principal coordinate analysis (PCoA) on the distance matrix
PBS_ordinate_PCoA_bray = ordinate(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_PBS, method = "PCoA", distance = "distance_bray_PBS")

PCoA_plot_PBS <- plot_ordination(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_PBS, PBS_ordinate_PCoA_bray, shape = "Sex", color = "Microniche")+
  theme_bw()+
  stat_ellipse()+ #adds an ellipse to a scatter plot to help visualize the distribution of points in the plot. The ellipse is drawn using a confidence interval, which can be specified using the level argument. By default, stat_ellipse() uses a 95% confidence interval.
   scale_color_manual(values = pastel_colors)  # Apply pastel colors

ord_PBS_df <- data.frame(sample_data(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_PBS))

# Run PERMANOVA using adonis2
PBS_perm_results <- adonis2(distance_bray_PBS ~ Microniche + Sex, data = ord_PBS_df, permutations = 999)

PBS_perm_results

#######################PBS########################
#Subset phyloseq object to only have the data from Bacteroides
btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_Bacteroides <- subset_samples(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed, 
                                                               Treatment == c( "Bacteroides"))

#Calculate Bray-Curtis dissimilarity distance matrix
distance_bray_Bacteroides = phyloseq::distance(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_Bacteroides, method = "bray")

#Perform a principal coordinate analysis (PCoA) on the distance matrix
Bacteroides_ordinate_PCoA_bray = ordinate(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_Bacteroides, method = "PCoA", distance = "distance_bray_Bacteroides")

PCoA_plot_Bacteroides <- plot_ordination(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_Bacteroides, Bacteroides_ordinate_PCoA_bray, shape = "Sex", color = "Microniche")+
  theme_bw()+
  stat_ellipse()+ #adds an ellipse to a scatter plot to help visualize the distribution of points in the plot. The ellipse is drawn using a confidence interval, which can be specified using the level argument. By default, stat_ellipse() uses a 95% confidence interval.
   scale_color_manual(values = pastel_colors)  # Apply pastel colors

ord_Bacteroides_df <- data.frame(sample_data(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_transformed_Bacteroides))

# Run PERMANOVA using adonis2
Bacteroides_perm_results <- adonis2(distance_bray_Bacteroides ~ Microniche + Sex, data = ord_Bacteroides_df, permutations = 999)

Bacteroides_perm_results

```


```{r}
# Access the taxonomy table
tax_table <- as.data.frame(tax_table(btphylo_nosacrifice))

# Extract the genera
genera <- tax_table$Genus

# Print unique genera
unique_genera <- unique(genera)
print(unique_genera)


```


#Plot relative abundance across gut
```{r}

#filter and Normalize the data for across the gut
wh02 = genefilter_sample(btphylo_atsacrifice, filterfun_sample(function(x) x > 1), A=0.1*nsamples(btphylo_atsacrifice))


btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned = prune_taxa(wh02, btphylo_atsacrifice)


#Aggregate Data at the Genus Level: You'll need to aggregate your OTU table at the genus level.
btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus <- tax_glom(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned, "Genus")

#Transform relative abundance
btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_norm100 <- transform_sample_counts(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus, function(x) x / sum(x) *100)

btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_norm100_relab_escherichia <- subset_taxa(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_norm100, Genus == "Escherichia-Shigella")

bt_genus_acrossgut_relab_escherichia_df <- psmelt(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_norm100_relab_escherichia)

bt_genus_acrossgut_relab_escherichia_lumen_df <- subset(bt_genus_acrossgut_relab_escherichia_df, Microniche == "LuminalContents")
bt_genus_acrossgut_relab_escherichia_mucosa_df <- subset(bt_genus_acrossgut_relab_escherichia_df, Microniche == "MucosalSurface")


escherichia_lumen_16s_plot_smooth_connected_points <- ggplot(bt_genus_acrossgut_relab_escherichia_lumen_df, aes(x = Biogeography, y = Abundance, color = Treatment, shape = Sex)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line(aes(group = interaction(Cage,Mouse)), size = 0.25) +  # Add this line to connect points for each MouseID
  stat_summary(fun.y = mean, size=2, geom = "line", aes(group = Treatment)) +
  scale_y_continuous(trans = 'identity') +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange")) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(y = "Relative Abundance of Escherichia (%)", x = "Experiment Day", color = "Treatment Group")

escherichia_lumen_16s_plot_smooth_connected_points


ggsave("Ecoli_acrossgut_lumen.pdf", plot = escherichia_lumen_16s_plot_smooth_connected_points, device = "pdf", width = 6, height = 3)

escherichia_mucosa_16s_plot_smooth_connected_points <- ggplot(bt_genus_acrossgut_relab_escherichia_mucosa_df, aes(x = Biogeography, y = Abundance, color = Treatment, shape = Sex)) +
    geom_point(size = 3) +  # Set size of the points
   geom_line(aes(group = interaction(Cage,Mouse_.)), size = 0.25) +  # Add this line to connect points for each MouseID
  stat_summary(fun.y = mean, size=2, geom = "line", aes(group = Treatment)) +
    stat_summary(fun = median, geom = "crossbar", aes(group = Treatment), position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), width = 0.5, fatten = 3) +  # Add median crossbars, position_dodge to separate them
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange")) +  # Consistent color scheme
    theme_bw() +
    labs(title = "Relative Abundance of Escherichia Across the Gut on the mucosa",
         x = "Biogeography",
         y = "Relative Abundance of Escherichia (%)")

escherichia_mucosa_16s_plot_smooth_connected_points

ggsave("Ecoli_acrossgut_mucoosa.pdf", plot = escherichia_mucosa_16s_plot_smooth_connected_points, device = "pdf", width = 6, height = 3)


```

#Plot relative abundance across the gut
```{r}

btphylo_atsacrifice

#filter and Normalize the data for across the gut
wh02 = genefilter_sample(btphylo_atsacrifice, filterfun_sample(function(x) x > 1), A=0.1*nsamples(btphylo_atsacrifice))


btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned = prune_taxa(wh02, btphylo_atsacrifice)


#Aggregate Data at the Genus Level: You'll need to aggregate your OTU table at the genus level.
btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus <- tax_glom(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned, "Genus")

#Transform relative abundance
btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_relab <- transform_sample_counts(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus, function(x) x / sum(x) *100)

#Extract and Prepare Data for Plotting:

btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_relab_df <- psmelt(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_relab)
btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_relab_df_bacteroides <- btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_relab_df %>% filter(Genus == "Bacteroides")

# Assuming YourDataFrame has a column named "Microniche"
btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_relab_df_bacteroides_lumen <- btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_relab_df_bacteroides %>%
    filter(Microniche == "LuminalContents")

btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_relab_df_bacteroides_mucosalsurface <- btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_relab_df_bacteroides %>%
    filter(Microniche == "MucosalSurface")

##Plot across the gut

Bt_16s_plot_acrossgut_biogeography_lumen <- ggplot(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_relab_df_bacteroides_lumen, aes(x = Biogeography, y = Abundance, color = Treatment, shape = Sex)) +
    geom_point(size = 3) +  # Set size of the points
   geom_line(aes(group = interaction(Cage,Mouse)), size = 0.25) +  # Add this line to connect points for each MouseID
  stat_summary(fun.y = mean, size=2, geom = "line", aes(group = Treatment)) +
    stat_summary(fun = median, geom = "crossbar", aes(group = Treatment), position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), width = 0.5, fatten = 3) +  # Add median crossbars, position_dodge to separate them
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange")) +  # Consistent color scheme
    theme_bw() +
    labs(x = "Biogeography",
         y = "Relative Abundance of Bacteroides (%)")+
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 70))  # Zoom into y-axis range


Bt_16s_plot_acrossgut_biogeography_lumen

ggsave("Bt_16s_plot_acrossgut_biogeography_lumen.pdf", plot = Bt_16s_plot_acrossgut_biogeography_lumen, device = "pdf", width = 6, height = 3)


#stats for bacteroides across gut in the lumen

btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_relab_df_bacteroides_lumen$MouseID <- paste(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_relab_df_bacteroides_lumen$Cage, btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_relab_df_bacteroides_lumen$Mouse_., sep = "_")


btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_relab_df_bacteroides_lumen$Treatment <- factor(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_relab_df_bacteroides_lumen$Treatment, levels=c("PBS", "Bacteroides", "Baseline"))

bacteroides_relabundance_acrossgut_lumen_model <- lmer(Abundance ~ Treatment + Biogeography + Sex + Treatment*Biogeography+(1|MouseID), data = btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_relab_df_bacteroides_lumen)

summary(bacteroides_relabundance_acrossgut_lumen_model)


```

#Plot across Microniches
```{r}


Bt_16s_plot_acrossgut_biogeography_mucosa <- ggplot(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_relab_df_bacteroides_mucosalsurface, aes(x = Biogeography, y = Abundance, color = Treatment, shape = Sex)) +
    geom_point(size = 3) +  # Set size of the points
   geom_line(aes(group = interaction(Cage,Mouse_.)), size = 0.25) +  # Add this line to connect points for each MouseID
  stat_summary(fun.y = mean, size=2, geom = "line", aes(group = Treatment)) +
    stat_summary(fun = median, geom = "crossbar", aes(group = Treatment), position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), width = 0.5, fatten = 3) +  # Add median crossbars, position_dodge to separate them
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange")) +  # Consistent color scheme
    theme_bw() +
    labs(x = "Biogeography",
         y = "Relative Abundance of Bacteroides (%)")+
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 70))  # Zoom into y-axis range


Bt_16s_plot_acrossgut_biogeography_mucosa

ggsave("Bt_16s_plot_acrossgut_biogeography_mucosa.pdf", plot = Bt_16s_plot_acrossgut_biogeography_mucosa, device = "pdf", width = 6, height = 3)

#stats for bacteroides across gut in the lumen

btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_relab_df_bacteroides_mucosalsurface$MouseID <- paste(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_relab_df_bacteroides_mucosalsurface$Cage, btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_relab_df_bacteroides_mucosalsurface$Mouse_., sep = "_")


btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_relab_df_bacteroides_mucosalsurface$Treatment <- factor(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_relab_df_bacteroides_mucosalsurface$Treatment, levels=c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides"))


btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_relab_df_bacteroides_mucosalsurface_model <- lmer(Abundance ~ Treatment + Biogeography + Sex + Treatment*Biogeography + (1|MouseID), data = btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_relab_df_bacteroides_mucosalsurface)

summary(btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned_genus_relab_df_bacteroides_mucosalsurface_model)





```


#Plot beta diversity between sexes
```{r}
# Compute ordination (PCoA) using a distance matrix (e.g., Morisita Horn)

btphylo_atsacrifice

btphylo_atsacrifice_normalized <- transform_sample_counts(btphylo_atsacrifice, function(x) x / sum(x) *100)

btphylo_atsacrifice_normalized_horn <- phyloseq::distance(btphylo_atsacrifice_normalized, method = "horn")

btphylo_atsacrifice_normalized_ord_pcoa_horn <- ordinate(btphylo_atsacrifice_normalized, method = "PCoA", distance = "horn")

# Plot the beta diversity between sexes, all together
betahorn_diversity_acrossgut_lumen_plot1 <- plot_ordination(btphylo_atsacrifice_normalized, btphylo_atsacrifice_normalized_ord_pcoa_horn, color = "Sex", shape = "Sex") +
  geom_point(size = 3) +  # Adjust size of points
  theme_bw() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("F" = "lightgreen", "M" = "lightblue"))+
  stat_ellipse(aes(group = Sex), linetype = 1)


betahorn_diversity_acrossgut_lumen_plot1

ggsave("betahorn_diversity_betwensexes_lumen_plot.pdf", plot = betahorn_diversity_acrossgut_lumen_plot1, device = "pdf", width = 6, height = 4)

# Plot the beta diversity between sexes, for each treatment
betahorn_diversity_acrossgut_lumen_plot2 <- plot_ordination(btEstablishmentWTmice_phyloseq_nobaselinemice_atsacrifice_lumen, ord_pcoa_horn, color = "Sex", shape = "Sex") +
  geom_point(size = 3) +  # Adjust size of points
  theme_bw() +
  facet_wrap(~Treatment)+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("F" = "lightgreen", "M" = "lightblue"))+
  stat_ellipse(aes(group = Sex), linetype = 1)


betahorn_diversity_acrossgut_lumen_plot2

ggsave("betahorn_diversity_betwensexes_lumen_plot2.pdf", plot = betahorn_diversity_acrossgut_lumen_plot2, device = "pdf", width = 6, height = 4)

# Compute ordination (PCoA) using a distance matrix (e.g., Bray-Curtis)
ord_pcoa_bray <- ordinate(btEstablishmentWTmice_phyloseq_nobaselinemice_atsacrifice_lumen, method = "PCoA", distance = "bray")

# Plot the beta diversity between sexes, all together
betabray_diversity_acrossgut_lumen_plot3 <- plot_ordination(btEstablishmentWTmice_phyloseq_nobaselinemice_atsacrifice_lumen, ord_pcoa_bray, color = "Sex", shape = "Sex") +
  geom_point(size = 3) +  # Adjust size of points
  theme_bw() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("F" = "lightgreen", "M" = "lightblue"))+
  stat_ellipse(aes(group = Sex), linetype = 1)


betabray_diversity_acrossgut_lumen_plot3

ggsave("betabray_diversity_betwensexes_lumen_plot3.pdf", plot = betabray_diversity_acrossgut_lumen_plot3, device = "pdf", width = 6, height = 4)

# Plot the beta diversity between sexes, for each treatment
betabray_diversity_acrossgut_lumen_plot4 <- plot_ordination(btEstablishmentWTmice_phyloseq_nobaselinemice_atsacrifice_lumen, ord_pcoa_bray, color = "Sex", shape = "Sex") +
  geom_point(size = 3) +  # Adjust size of points
  theme_bw() +
  facet_wrap(~Treatment)+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("F" = "lightgreen", "M" = "lightblue"))+
  stat_ellipse(aes(group = Sex), linetype = 1)


betahorn_diversity_acrossgut_lumen_plot4

ggsave("betabray_diversity_betwensexes_lumen_plot4.pdf", plot = betabray_diversity_acrossgut_lumen_plot4, device = "pdf", width = 6, height = 4)
```

```{r}

# Plot the beta diversity with regions all togther but treatments separated
betahorn_diversity_acrossgut_lumen_plot2 <- plot_ordination(btEstablishmentWTmice_phyloseq_nobaselinemice_atsacrifice_lumen, ord_pcoa_horn, color = "Biogeography", shape = "Sex") +
  geom_point(size = 3) +  # Adjust size of points
  theme_bw() +
  facet_wrap(~Treatment, nrow = 1, ncol = 3) +  # Facet by Biogeography with specified layout
  scale_color_manual(values = c("SmallIntestine" = "darkblue", "Cecum" = "royalblue", "ProximalColon" = "lightblue", "DistalColon" = "#D1E8EE"))+
  stat_ellipse(aes(group = Biogeography), linetype = 1)


betahorn_diversity_acrossgut_lumen_plot2

ggsave("betahorn_diversity_acrossgut_pertreatment_lumen_plot.pdf", plot = betahorn_diversity_acrossgut_lumen_plot2, device = "pdf", width = 8, height = 2)


# Plot the beta diversity with biogeography faceted
betahorn_diversity_acrossgut_lumen_plot <- plot_ordination(btEstablishmentWTmice_phyloseq_nobaselinemice_atsacrifice_lumen, ord_pcoa_horn, color = "Treatment", shape = "Sex") +
  geom_point(size = 3) +  # Adjust size of points
  facet_wrap(~Biogeography, nrow = 1, ncol = 4) +  # Facet by Biogeography with specified layout
  theme_bw() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"))+
  stat_ellipse(aes(group = Treatment), linetype = 1)


betahorn_diversity_acrossgut_lumen_plot

ggsave("betahorn_diversity_acrossgut_lumen_plot.pdf", plot = betahorn_diversity_acrossgut_lumen_plot, device = "pdf", width = 8, height = 2)



# Compute ordination (PCoA) using a distance matrix (e.g., Morisita)

ord_pcoa_bray <- ordinate(btEstablishmentWTmice_phyloseq_nobaselinemice_atsacrifice_lumen, method = "PCoA", distance = "bray")

# Plot the beta diversity
betabray_diversity_acrossgut_lumen_plot <- plot_ordination(btEstablishmentWTmice_phyloseq_nobaselinemice_atsacrifice_lumen, ord_pcoa_bray, color = "Treatment", shape = "Sex") +
  geom_point(size = 3) +  # Adjust size of points
  theme_bw() +
  facet_wrap(~Biogeography, nrow = 1, ncol = 4) +  # Facet by Biogeography with specified layout
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"))+
  stat_ellipse(aes(group = Treatment), linetype = 1)


betabray_diversity_acrossgut_lumen_plot


```

#Plot beta diversity across the gut, coloring by treatment group

```{r}
desired_timepoints <- c("Sacrifice", "LPS", "Baseline_6")
desired_treatments <- c("Baseline")

phylo_LPSandSacrifice <- subset_samples(btEstablishmentWTmice_phyloseq, 
                                        Timepoint %in% desired_timepoints & 
                                        (Treatment %in% desired_treatments | Timepoint != "Baseline_6"))

# Extract the sample data from the phyloseq object
sample_data_df <- as(sample_data(phylo_LPSandSacrifice), "data.frame")

sample_data_df <- sample_data_df %>% 
  mutate(Biogeography = case_when(
    is.na(Biogeography) ~ "Fecal",
    TRUE ~ Biogeography  # Keep all other values as is
  ))

#Factor order of biogrography
sample_data_df$Biogeography <- factor(sample_data_df$Biogeography, levels = c("SmallIntestine", "Cecum", "ProximalColon", "DistalColon", "Fecal"))


# Update the sample data in the phyloseq object
sample_data(phylo_LPSandSacrifice) <- sample_data_df

#filter the data for beta diverity analysis


#selecting taxa that are present in more than 1 count in at least 10% of the samples. This is a common way to filter out low-abundance taxa that might be less informative or represent sequencing noise
wh03 = genefilter_sample(phylo_LPSandSacrifice, filterfun_sample(function(x) x > 1), A=0.1*nsamples(phylo_LPSandSacrifice))


#removes samples that have zero total counts. 
phylo_LPSandSacrifice_toprune <- prune_samples(sample_sums(phylo_LPSandSacrifice) > 0, phylo_LPSandSacrifice)

#applying the filter criteria from step 1 to prune the taxa from the phylo_LPSandSacrifice_toprune object. This results in phylo_LPSandSacrifice_pruned, which contains only the taxa that meet your filtering criteria
phylo_LPSandSacrifice_pruned = prune_taxa(wh03, phylo_LPSandSacrifice_toprune)




#This is the ordination with the normalized data. You usually shouldn't calculate beta diversity distances on normalized data.
phylo_LPSandSacrifice_pruned_ord_horn = ordinate(phylo_LPSandSacrifice_pruned, method="PCoA", distance = "horn")

#PCoA is 35.3%
