```{r}
phylo_LPSandSacrifice_beta_horn <- plot_ordination(phylo_LPSandSacrifice, phylo_LPSandSacrifice_pruned_ord_horn, color = "Biogeography", shape = "Sex") +
  geom_point(size = 2) +  # Adjust size of points
  theme_bw() +
  facet_wrap(~Treatment, nrow = 1, ncol = 3) +  # Facet by Biogeography with specified layout
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Fecal" = "darkgreen", "SmallIntestine" = "darkblue", "Cecum" = "royalblue", "ProximalColon" = "lightblue", "DistalColon" = "#D1E8EE"))+
  stat_ellipse(aes(group = Biogeography), linetype = 1)

phylo_LPSandSacrifice_beta_horn

ggsave("phylo_LPSandSacrifice_beta_horn_acrosstreatment.pdf", width = 10, height = 3)


phylo_LPSandSacrifice_no_fecal <- subset_samples(phylo_LPSandSacrifice, Biogeography != "Fecal")
phylo_LPSandSacrifice_no_fecal_ord_horn <- ordinate(phylo_LPSandSacrifice_no_fecal, method = "PCoA", distance = "horn")

phylo_LPSandSacrifice_acrossgut_beta_horn <- plot_ordination(phylo_LPSandSacrifice_no_fecal, phylo_LPSandSacrifice_no_fecal_ord_horn, color = "Treatment", shape = "Microniche") +
  geom_jitter(size = 2) +  # Add jitter to points
  theme_bw() +
  facet_wrap(~Biogeography, nrow = 1, ncol = 4) +  # Facet by Biogeography with specified layout
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "lightgreen", "PBS" = "lightblue", "Bacteroides" = "orange"))+
  stat_ellipse(aes(group = Treatment), linetype = 1)

phylo_LPSandSacrifice_acrossgut_beta_horn

ggsave("phylo_LPSandSacrifice_beta_horn_acrossgut.pdf", width = 10, height = 3)


```



##### Running statistics forphylo_LPSandSacrifice_acrossgut_beta_horn
```{r}


# Assuming phylo_LPSandSacrifice_pruned is your phyloseq object
biogeographies <- c("SmallIntestine", "Cecum", "ProximalColon", "DistalColon")
results <- list()
pairwise_results <- list()


for (biogeo in biogeographies) {
  # Subset the phyloseq object
  phy_subset <- subset_samples(phylo_LPSandSacrifice_pruned, Biogeography == biogeo)
  
  # Create a distance matrix
  dist_matrix <- distance(phy_subset, method = "horn") # Replace 'bray' with your preferred distance method

  # Convert sample data to a data frame and ensure the presence of Treatment and Sex columns
  df_sample_data <- data.frame(sample_data(phy_subset))

  # Check if 'Treatment' and 'Sex' columns exist in the data frame
  if(!("Treatment" %in% colnames(df_sample_data)) || !("Sex" %in% colnames(df_sample_data))) {
    stop("The required columns 'Treatment' and 'Sex' are not present in the sample data.")
  }

  # Run PERMANOVA using adonis2
  perm_result <- adonis2(dist_matrix ~ Treatment + Sex + Microniche, data = df_sample_data, permutations = 999)
  results[[biogeo]] <- perm_result
  
   # Perform pairwise Adonis
  pairwise_result <- pairwise.adonis(dist_matrix, df_sample_data$Treatment)
  pairwise_results[[biogeo]] <- pairwise_result
}

# View results
results
pairwise_results

```

#Run another permanova for across treatments
```{r}

desired_timepoints2 <- c("Sacrifice", "LPS")

phylo_LPSandSacrifice2 <- subset_samples(btEstablishmentWTmice_phyloseq, 
                                        Timepoint %in% desired_timepoints2)
sample_data_df <- sample_data_df %>% 
  mutate(Biogeography = case_when(
    is.na(Biogeography) ~ "Fecal",
    TRUE ~ Biogeography  # Keep all other values as is
  ))

#Factor order of biogrography
sample_data_df$Biogeography <- factor(sample_data_df$Biogeography, levels = c("SmallIntestine", "Cecum", "ProximalColon", "DistalColon", "Fecal"))


# Update the sample data in the phyloseq object
sample_data(phylo_LPSandSacrifice2) <- sample_data_df

#filter the data for beta diverity analysis


#selecting taxa that are present in more than 1 count in at least 10% of the samples. This is a common way to filter out low-abundance taxa that might be less informative or represent sequencing noise
wh04 = genefilter_sample(phylo_LPSandSacrifice2, filterfun_sample(function(x) x > 1), A=0.1*nsamples(phylo_LPSandSacrifice))


#removes samples that have zero total counts. 
phylo_phylo_LPSandSacrifice2_toprune <- prune_samples(sample_sums(phylo_LPSandSacrifice2) > 0, phylo_LPSandSacrifice2)

#applying the filter criteria from step 1 to prune the taxa from the phylo_LPSandSacrifice_toprune object. This results in phylo_LPSandSacrifice_pruned, which contains only the taxa that meet your filtering criteria
phylo_phylo_LPSandSacrifice2_pruned = prune_taxa(wh04, phylo_phylo_LPSandSacrifice2_toprune)

library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)  # For adonis2
# Assuming the pairwise.adonis function is correctly loaded from its source

Treatments <- c("Baseline", "PBS", "Bacteroides")
results2 <- list()
pairwise_results <- list()

for (Treat in Treatments) {
  # Subset the phyloseq object based on the Treatment
  phy_subset <- subset_samples(phylo_phylo_LPSandSacrifice2_pruned, Treatment == Treat)

  # Ensure there are enough samples to analyze
  if (nsamples(phy_subset) > 1) {
    # Convert sample data to a data frame
    df_sample_data <- as.data.frame(sample_data(phy_subset))

    # Check if the necessary columns are present
    if (!all(c("Biogeography", "Sex", "Microniche") %in% colnames(df_sample_data))) {
      stop("One or more required columns (Biogeography, Sex, Microniche) are missing in the sample data.")
    }

    # Create a distance matrix
    dist_matrix <- phyloseq::distance(phy_subset, method = "horn")

    # Run PERMANOVA using adonis2
    perm_result <- adonis2(dist_matrix ~ Biogeography + Sex + Microniche, 
                           data = df_sample_data, permutations = 999)
    results[[Treat]] <- perm_result

    # Perform pairwise Adonis - modify as per your actual requirements
    pairwise_result2 <- pairwise.adonis(dist_matrix, df_sample_data$Biogeography)
    pairwise_results2[[Treat]] <- pairwise_result2
  } else {
    cat("Not enough samples found for Treatment:", Treat, "\n")
  }
}

# View results
print(results2)
print(pairwise_results)



```



#Run an analysis that identifies what microbes are most different between each region
```{r}

library(phyloseq)

 (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly=TRUE))
    install.packages("BiocManager")
  
BiocManager::install("ANCOMBC", force = TRUE)

library(ANCOMBC)
library(dplyr)
library(nloptr)

#loading example data so that i can see what this looks like
data(dietswap)

#Data from samples collected across gut at sacrifice
btphylo_atsacrifice <- subset_samples(btEstablishmentWTmice_phyloseq, Timepoint == "Sacrifice")

#wh0_btphylo_atsacrifice = genefilter_sample(btphylo_atsacrifice, filterfun_sample(function(x) x > 1), A=0.1*nsamples(btphylo_atsacrifice))

#btphylo_atsacrifice_pruned = prune_taxa(wh0_btphylo_atsacrifice, btphylo_atsacrifice)


btphylo_atsacrifice_relab100<- transform_sample_counts(btphylo_atsacrifice, function(x) x / sum(x) *100)

# Ensure that the taxonomy table has a 'Genus' level
if ("Genus" %in% rank_names(btphylo_atsacrifice_relab100)) {
    # Aggregate by Genus
    btphylo_atsacrifice_relab100_genus <- tax_glom(btphylo_atsacrifice_relab100, "Genus")
} else {
    stop("The taxonomy table does not contain a 'Genus' level. Please check your data.")
}

# Extract the data from the phyloseq object
ASV_data <- as(otu_table(btphylo_atsacrifice_relab100_genus), "matrix")
tax_table <- as.data.frame(tax_table(btphylo_atsacrifice_relab100_genus))
sample_data <- sample_data(btphylo_atsacrifice_relab100_genus)

# Convert data to a dataframe for ANCOMBC
ASV_data_df <- data.frame(t(ASV_data))

# Check if all ASVs in df_data are present in tax_table
if (!all(colnames(ASV_data_df) %in% rownames(tax_table))) {
    stop("Some ASVs in df_data are not present in tax_table.")
}

genus_names <- tax_table[match(colnames(ASV_data_df), rownames(tax_table)), "Genus"]

# Replace missing or NA genus names with a placeholder or unique identifier
genus_names[is.na(genus_names)] <- paste("NA", seq(along=genus_names[is.na(genus_names)]), sep="_")

colnames(ASV_data_df) <- genus_names



sample_data$SampleID <- rownames(sample_data)
grp.names <- as.vector(sample_data$Treatment)

df_metadata <- data.frame(sample_data)

#feature.table: Data frame or matrix representing observed OTU table with OTUs (or taxa) in rows and samples in columns.
df_data

#meta.data: Data frame or matrix of all variables and covariates of interest.
sample_data

#sample.var: Character. The name of column storing sample IDs.
SampleID

#group.var: Character. The name of the main variable of interest. ANCOM-BC v1.0 only supports discrete group.var and aims to compare the change of absolute abundance across different levels of group.var.
grp.names


#zero.cut: Numerical fraction between 0 and 1. Taxa with proportion of zeroes greater than zero.cut are not included in the analysis.
0.01

#lib.cut: Numeric. Samples with library size less than lib.cut are not included in the analysis.


#neg.lb: Logical. TRUE indicates a taxon would be classified as a structural zero in the corresponding experimental group using its asymptotic lower bound.

ANCOM_BC(feature.table, 
grp.name, 
grp.ind, 
struc.zero, 
adj.method, tol.EM, max.iterNum, perNum, alpha)






#Run ANCOM-BC
ancombc(
  data = NULL,
  assay.type = NULL,
  assay_name = "counts",
  rank = NULL,
  tax_level = NULL,
  phyloseq = btphylo_atsacrifice_relab100_genus,
  formula,
  p_adj_method = "holm",
  prv_cut = 0.1,
  lib_cut = 0,
  group = NULL,
  struc_zero = FALSE,
  neg_lb = FALSE,
  tol = 1e-05,
  max_iter = 100,
  conserve = FALSE,
  alpha = 0.05,
  global = FALSE,
  n_cl = 1,
  verbose = FALSE
)
```


#Using biomehorizon to look at differential abundance across time


```{r}

## Install devtools
install.packages("devtools")


devtools::install_github("blekhmanlab/biomehorizon")
library(biomehorizon)  
otusample_diet <- otusample_diet

otusample_diet %>%
	arrange(desc(MCT.f.0002)) %>%
	select(1:7) %>%
	head()

head(metadatasample_diet)

metadatasample_diet <- metadatasample_diet

length(metadatasample_diet$subject[metadatasample_diet$subject == "MCTs01"])

paramList <- prepanel(otudata = otusample_diet, metadata = metadatasample_diet, subj = "MCTs01")


library(dplyr)

## Retrieve samples from MCTs01
otusample_subj1 <- otusample_diet %>%
	select(taxon_id, as.character((metadatasample_diet %>% filter(subject=="MCTs01"))$sample))
	

otusample_subj1 %>%
	filter(taxon_id == "taxon 2") %>%
	select(-taxon_id) %>%
	as.numeric()

paramList <- prepanel(otudata = otusample_diet, metadata = metadatasample_diet, subj = "MCTs01",
thresh_prevalence = 90, thresh_abundance = 1.5)


paramList <- prepanel(otudata = otusample_diet, metadata = metadatasample_diet, subj = "MCTs01",
otulist = c("taxon 1", "taxon 2", "taxon 10", "taxon 14"))

## Basic plot using default filtering thresholds
paramList <- prepanel(otudata = otusample_diet, metadata = metadatasample_diet, subj = "MCTs01")

horizonplot(paramList)

```
